data.title: Write failing integration tests for Confluence integration
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Failing integration test suite that defines the acceptance contract for the Confluence integration before any implementation begins.
**Why**: Establishes observable, runnable verification criteria for all three integration capabilities (researcher, cache, citation injection) — ensuring implementation stories have a concrete RED baseline to satisfy.
**Scope**:
- IN: tests/integration/test-confluence-integration.sh using a local HTTP stub fixture (no live Confluence required)
- IN: Test (a) — researcher returns at least 1 of top 3 summaries containing a keyword from the seed topic when given a stub CQL response
- IN: Test (b) — confluence-fetch.sh serves content from disk and emits "[cache hit: <url>]" to stderr for files written within the last hour; makes no network request on cache hit
- IN: Test (c) — ref-library-loader.sh annotates at least one task with "> Reference: [Doc Title](url) — [one-sentence excerpt]" when given a reference fixture whose topic tags match the task
- OUT: Implementation of confluence-fetch.sh, dso:confluence-researcher, or ref-library-loader.sh (those are implementation stories)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, tests/integration/test-confluence-integration.sh exists and covers tests (a), (b), and (c) using a local HTTP stub fixture
  ← Satisfies: "An integration test suite in tests/integration/test-confluence-integration.sh validates using a local HTTP stub fixture"
- When this story is complete, running the test suite confirms all three tests FAIL (RED) — the failing run result is recorded as a ticket comment on this story
  ← Satisfies: RED acceptance criteria — tests must be confirmed failing before implementation begins

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
